description: Contrastive pair datasets for CAZ (Concept Assembly Zone) empirical validation
format: JSONL — one record per line, two records per pair (label 1 = positive class, label 0 = negative class)

fields.label: 1 = positive class (affirming/epistemic/causal/etc), 0 = negative class
fields.domain: Topic domain: science, everyday, history, technology (25 pairs per domain)
fields.model_name: Generation model (HuggingFace or FuelIX model ID)
fields.concept: Concept being tested (may be absent in older datasets)

credibility_pairs.jsonl.description: Credible vs non-credible statements — tests epistemic signal assembly
credibility_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Non-credible, poorly-evidenced, or epistemically suspect statement
credibility_pairs.jsonl.notes: Original dataset, generated before concept field was added. Domain names differ from later datasets.

negation_pairs.jsonl.description: Affirmative vs negated statements — tests syntactic negation signal assembly
negation_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Negated statement (explicit negation markers: not, never, no, cannot)
negation_pairs.jsonl.notes: Expanded from original 20-pair pilot to 100 pairs on 2026-03-14. Original pilot used Ollama/local models.

sentiment_pairs.jsonl.description: Positive vs negative sentiment statements — tests affective signal assembly
sentiment_pairs.jsonl.notes: Regenerated to full 100 pairs on 2026-03-14 (was 99 pairs due to one dropped record).

certainty_pairs.jsonl.description: High-certainty vs uncertain statements — tests epistemic certainty distinct from credibility
certainty_pairs.jsonl.label_1: High-certainty statement ('I know', 'it is clear that', 'definitely')
certainty_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Uncertain statement ('I'm not sure', 'it is unclear', 'the evidence is mixed')
certainty_pairs.jsonl.notes: history_25 (Magna Carta topic) regenerated separately after rate-limit drop during initial generation run.

plurality_pairs.jsonl.description: Plural vs singular grammatical constructions — tests syntactic number signal
plurality_pairs.jsonl.label_1: Plural constructions throughout ('researchers have', 'the studies show')
plurality_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Singular constructions throughout ('the researcher has', 'the study shows')
plurality_pairs.jsonl.notes: Same factual content in both members of each pair; only grammatical number varies.

causation_pairs.jsonl.description: Causal vs non-causal relationship statements — tests relational/causal signal
causation_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Non-causal framing ('coincided with', 'was observed alongside', 'does not cause')
causation_pairs.jsonl.notes: Relational concept — expected to sit between syntactic and epistemic in CAZ depth ordering.

moral_valence_pairs.jsonl.description: Moral approval vs moral condemnation — tests moral evaluation distinct from sentiment
moral_valence_pairs.jsonl.label_1: Morally positive evaluation ('morally right', 'ethical', 'we have a duty to')
moral_valence_pairs.jsonl.notes: Distinct from sentiment: focuses on moral/ethical evaluation rather than emotional tone.

temporal_order_pairs.jsonl.description: Forward vs reversed temporal sequence — tests temporal ordering signal
temporal_order_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Reversed or unclear temporal order (opposite sequence or 'the order is disputed')
temporal_order_pairs.jsonl.notes: Hit FuelIX rate limits during generation; backoff recovery worked, all 100 pairs complete.

caz_predictions.validated_at: GPT-2-XL (48 layers) for negation, sentiment, credibility
